titleStmt.title: Letter from Pat S. Higgins to the Under-Secretary, 13 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick S. Higgins

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Patrick S. Higgins, Acting Town Clerk, Waterford, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, submitting an application for the reimbursement of court expenses incurred by the Corporation for the year to 31 March 1916.Sir Robert Chalmers was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/12088

correspAction.placeName: Town Clerk's Office, Town Hall, Waterford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Clerks Office Town Hall Waterford 13th July, 1916 Sir, I beg to forward, for the purpose of
recoupment, the accompanying Statement showing the
amounts advanced by the Corporation during the
financial year ended the 31 st March, 1916, in respect
of expenses of Prosecutors and Witnesses, together
with Petty Sessions Certificates, Paying Orders
issued by the Cerk of the Crown and Peace and the
certificates of the Crown Solicitor. I am to request you to be good enough to
return me the documents when no longer requrred and
to acknowledge their receipt. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, Pat S Higgins
Acting Town Clerk. The Under Secretary, The Castle, DUBLIN.
